title: Essentially Optimized
description: A lightweight Minecraft modpack focused on performance optimization and essential quality-of-life improvements, while staying true to the vanilla experience.
body:
# **Essentially Optimized Modpack**

Essentially Optimized is a performance-focused and quality-of-life modpack for Minecraft Fabric 1.21.11. It is designed to boost FPS, reduce lag, and enhance your gameplay experience while keeping Minecraft visually smooth and responsive.

>  Key Features

- Performance Boost:
With mods like Sodium, Lithium, FerriteCore, Entity Culling, and More Culling, you can expect an FPS increase of 67–80% in most worlds, depending on your system and world size.

- Quality of Life Enhancements:
Optional mods like Enchant Icons, Re-covered (Enchanted Books), Enchantment Glows, Recolourful Containers GUI + HUD, Fancy Crops, and Connected Paths improve inventory management, enchantment visuals, and general gameplay convenience.

- Client Visual Improvements:
Resource packs and visual tweaks like Zoomify, Dynamic FPS, AppleSkin, and Theone’s Eating Animation Pack enhance game aesthetics while keeping performance high.

>  Included Mods & Resource Packs

- Required for Performance: Sodium, Lithium, FerriteCore, Entity Culling, More Culling, BadOptimizations, C2ME, ModernFix-mVUS, Dynamic FPS, Debugify, Krypton, Fast IP Ping, ScalableLux, VMP, Zoomify, Mouse Tweaks, Shulker Box Tooltip, BetterF3, Cloth Config API, Mod Menu, Fabric API, Fabric Language Kotlin, ImmediatelyFast, Reese’s Sodium Options, Continuity

- Optional / Client-side Enhancements: Enchant Icons, Re-covered, Enchantment Glows, Recolourful Containers GUI + HUD, Fancy Crops, Connected Paths, LowOnFire, Low Shield, Theone’s Eating Animation Pack

>  System Requirements

- Loader: Fabric 1.21.11

- Game Version: 1.21.11

- Recommended: 4GB+ RAM for optimal performance

- Can be used in Singleplayer and on vanilla servers

>  Notes

- Optional mods and resource packs are client-side only; install only if desired.

Performance boost will vary depending on system specs, world size, and other installed mods.
